terms.definition: The procedure of choosing parameters θ that maximize L(θ) = P(data | θ), equivalently θ̂ = argmax_θ ℓ(θ) for log-likelihood ℓ.
Abbreviation for maximum likelihood estimation or the resulting estimate θ̂_MLE.
ℓ(θ) = log L(θ) = Σᵢ log P(xᵢ | θ); maximized at the same θ as L(θ) because log is strictly increasing.
−ℓ(θ); minimized during training when MLE is performed numerically; common classification and regression losses are NLL under stated models.
The argument (parameter value) that achieves the maximum of a function; θ̂_MLE = argmax_θ L(θ).
